_note: Zarma (zar) — values are English placeholders pending professional translation.

meta.title: Muriyar Ta
meta.description: Amplifying girls' voices through anonymous storytelling, digital media, and advocacy.

adminPodcast.dashSubtitle: Create, edit, and publish podcast episodes.
adminPodcast.emptyFreshBody: No podcast episodes have been created yet. Create your first episode to begin publishing audio stories.
adminPodcast.emptyFilteredBody: No episodes match your current filters. Try adjusting your search or clearing one of the filters.
adminPodcast.loadError: Could not load episodes. Please try again.
adminPodcast.editorSubtitle: Update metadata, then save, publish, or unpublish.
adminPodcast.notFoundBody: This episode may have been removed or never existed.
adminPodcast.fieldSlugHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes. Up to 80 characters.
adminPodcast.featuredCaptionWhenDraft: Featured can only be set on published episodes.
adminPodcast.featuredCaptionWhenPublished: Mark this as the featured episode on the public podcast page.
adminPodcast.publishHint: Publishing is available only while the episode is a draft.
adminPodcast.unpublishHint: Unpublishing is available only while the episode is published.

errors.rpc_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.invalid_payload: Some fields in this form aren't recognized.
errors.slug_format: Slug must use lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes only (1–80 characters).
errors.slug_taken: This slug is already in use by another episode.
errors.podcast_description_too_long: Description is too long (up to 1000 characters).
errors.podcast_summary_too_long: Episode summary is too long (up to 8000 characters).
errors.podcast_featured_requires_published: Only published episodes can be featured.
errors.podcast_not_draft: This episode is not a draft, so it can't be published from here.
errors.podcast_not_published: This episode is not currently published.

podcast.metaDescription: Anonymous stories, expert conversation, and the topics communities rarely discuss aloud.
podcast.heroBody: Anonymous stories from girls and young women, brought together with expert voices, taboo-breaking conversations, and roundtables with the next generation — across Niger and West Africa.
podcast.featuredEmptyTitle: A featured episode will appear here soon
podcast.featuredEmptyBody: When the first episodes are published, one will be highlighted here as a starting point.
podcast.seriesDescription: Four editorial series, each with a distinct purpose.
podcast.themesDescription: Topics the podcast explores. New themes appear here as episodes are tagged.
podcast.themesEmpty: Themes will appear here as episodes are published.
podcast.languagesDescription: Listen in the language closest to home.
podcast.latestEmptyBody: When the first episodes are published, they'll show up here, newest first.

anonymous-voices.name: Anonymous Voices
anonymous-voices.tagline: One anonymous story, in her own words.
anonymous-voices.description: Our flagship series. Each episode tells one anonymous story, carefully de-identified and shared with consent.

beyond-the-story.name: Beyond the Story
beyond-the-story.tagline: An anonymous story, with experts to help us understand.
beyond-the-story.description: Each episode begins with an anonymous story, then brings together psychologists, lawyers, teachers, researchers, and activists to ask why this happens — and what we can do.

breaking-the-silence.name: Breaking the Silence
breaking-the-silence.tagline: Conversations on topics communities rarely discuss aloud.
breaking-the-silence.description: Child marriage, menstruation, consent, mental health, body image, puberty, domestic violence, friendship — talked about openly, with care.

youth-voices.name: Youth Voices
youth-voices.description: Roundtables between young people on education, leadership, university, marriage, social media, dreams, and community change.

footer.crisisBody: This platform reviews stories asynchronously and cannot respond to emergencies. If you are in danger, contact local emergency services or a support organization.
footer.mission: A safe, anonymous space where girls and young women can share their stories, be heard, and help shape change.
footer.contactBody: NGOs, researchers, educators, and policymakers are welcome to reach out.
footer.tagline: Inspiring advocacy and change through lived experiences.

errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.body: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errors.notFoundBody: The page you are looking for does not exist.

stories.listSubtitle: Real experiences, carefully de-identified and shared with consent.
stories.emptyBody: When stories are published, they'll appear here.
stories.notFoundBody: This story may have been removed or is not yet published.

resources.listSubtitle: A curated directory of support for girls and young women — across education, health, legal aid, and crisis services.
resources.crisisSubtitle: Immediate support reachable now. If you are in danger, please contact local emergency services.
resources.crisisEmptyTitle: Crisis resources will appear here soon
resources.crisisEmptyBody: We are curating verified crisis support. In the meantime, please contact your local emergency services.
resources.emptyBody: Verified resources will appear here once they are added.
resources.emptyBodySearch: No resources match “{q}”. Try a different term or clear the search.

playerUnavailableBody: This episode does not yet have a playable audio source. The transcript below is the canonical version of the episode.
transcriptEmptyBody: Transcripts are an essential part of Muriyar Ta. This episode's transcript will appear here as soon as it is ready.
relatedStoryDescription: This episode begins with an anonymous story shared on Muriyar Ta. Read it in the contributor's own words.
relatedResourcesDescription: Curated support connected to the themes of this episode.
notFoundBody: This episode may have been removed, archived, or is not yet published.
podcastDescription: This story has inspired one or more conversations on the Muriyar Ta podcast.
resourcesDescription: Curated organizations connected to the themes of this story.
